name: Story Sinks
description: Four SinkProtocol implementations for emitting closed stories: (1) disk.py — writes closed stories as append-only JSONL with async file I/O and read-back deserialization, (2) stigmergy.py — placeholder that emits stories as Signals to Stigmergy, (3) apprentice.py — placeholder that emits stories as training sequences, (4) kindex.py — filtered emission of noteworthy stories as knowledge nodes based on configurable thresholds (story_type, min event count, duration). All sinks are fire-and-forget with error propagation to the orchestrator's safe_emit wrapper; a failing sink never blocks others or crashes the engine. Covers AC7 (disk persistence), AC8 (sink fan-out), AC17 (kindex filtering).

types.name: SinkName
types.description: Canonical names for the four built-in sink implementations.

types.name: DiskSinkConfig

fields.name: path
fields.description: Filesystem path to the JSONL output file for closed stories.

fields.name: flush_on_write
fields.description: Whether to flush the file handle after each write. Defaults to true for durability.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic configuration model for the disk JSONL sink.

validators.error_message: File path must be between 1 and 4096 characters.
File path must not be blank/whitespace-only.

types.name: KindexSinkConfig

fields.name: story_types
fields.description: If set, only stories whose story_type is in this set are considered noteworthy. None means all story types qualify.

fields.name: min_event_count
fields.description: Minimum number of events a story must contain to be noteworthy.

fields.name: min_duration_seconds
fields.description: Minimum duration in seconds between opened_at and closed_at for a story to be noteworthy.

types.description: Frozen Pydantic configuration model for the kindex knowledge-capture sink. Defines thresholds that determine whether a story is 'noteworthy' enough to emit as a knowledge node (per AS12).

types.name: OptionalStringSet
types.description: An optional set of strings. None means 'no filter applied'.

types.name: SinkEmitResult
types.description: Outcome of a single sink emit attempt within the safe_emit orchestrator wrapper.

types.name: SafeEmitOutcome

fields.name: sink_name
fields.description: Canonical name of the sink that was invoked.

fields.name: story_id
fields.description: ID of the story that was emitted (or attempted).

fields.name: result
fields.description: Whether the emit succeeded, was filtered, or errored.

fields.name: error_message
fields.description: Error detail if result is 'error', empty string otherwise.

types.description: Result record from the safe_emit orchestrator wrapper for one sink invocation.

types.name: Story

fields.name: events
fields.description: Ordered list of correlated Event objects.

fields.name: opened_at
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the story was opened.

fields.name: closed_at
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the story was closed.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the story.

types.description: Reference to schemas.Story — the closed story object that sinks receive. Defined in the schemas component; referenced here for type completeness.

functions.name: DiskSink.start
functions.description: Opens the JSONL output file handle via aiofiles for the lifetime of the sink. Must be called before any emit() calls. Acquires the internal asyncio.Lock.

error_cases.name: file_open_error
error_cases.condition: The configured path cannot be opened for writing (permissions, missing directory, disk full).

error_cases.name: already_started
error_cases.condition: start() is called when the file handle is already open.

preconditions: DiskSink was constructed with a valid DiskSinkConfig.
start() has not already been called (no double-start).
postconditions: An aiofiles file handle is open in append mode at the configured path.
The internal asyncio.Lock is initialized.
The sink is ready to accept emit() calls.

structured_side_effects.description: Opens (or creates) the JSONL file in append mode.
Logs file open at INFO level.

functions.name: DiskSink.emit
functions.description: Serializes a closed Story to JSON and appends it as a single line to the JSONL file. Write is protected by an asyncio.Lock to ensure atomicity of the line write. Errors propagate to the caller (the orchestrator's safe_emit wrapper).

error_cases.name: serialization_error
error_cases.condition: The story cannot be serialized to JSON (should not happen with valid Pydantic models).

error_cases.name: write_error
error_cases.condition: The filesystem write fails (disk full, handle closed unexpectedly, I/O error).

preconditions: start() has been called and the file handle is open.
story is a valid, closed Story object.
postconditions: The story's JSON representation followed by a newline has been appended to the file.
If flush_on_write is true, the file buffer has been flushed to disk.

structured_side_effects.description: Appends one JSON line (story) to the JSONL file.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is the number of events in the story

functions.name: DiskSink.close
functions.description: Flushes and closes the JSONL file handle. After close(), no further emit() calls are valid.

error_cases.name: close_error
error_cases.condition: The file handle close operation fails.

postconditions: The file handle is flushed and closed.
Any subsequent emit() call will raise RuntimeError.

structured_side_effects.description: Flushes remaining buffer and closes the file handle.
Logs file close at INFO level.

functions.name: read_stories
functions.description: Standalone async generator that reads a JSONL file and yields deserialized Story objects. Corrupted lines are logged at WARNING and skipped — never raises for bad data. This is not part of SinkProtocol; it is a utility function in disk.py for read-back.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: Path to the JSONL file to read.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: The specified path does not exist.

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: The process lacks read permissions for the file.

preconditions: The file at path exists and is readable.
postconditions: Every valid JSON line in the file is yielded as a Story object.
Corrupted or unparseable lines are skipped with a warning log.
The file handle is closed after iteration completes or on error.

structured_side_effects.description: Opens and reads the JSONL file line by line.
Logs corrupted lines at WARNING level with line number and error detail.

functions.name: StigmergySink.start
functions.description: No-op initialization for the Stigmergy placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is starting.
postconditions: The sink is marked as started.

functions.name: StigmergySink.emit
functions.description: Placeholder: logs at DEBUG level the story_id and story_type that would be emitted as a Signal to Stigmergy. In a real implementation, this would make an HTTP POST to the Stigmergy service.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The closed story to emit as a Signal.

postconditions: A DEBUG log line has been emitted describing the story that would be sent.

structured_side_effects.description: Logs placeholder emit at DEBUG: story_id, story_type.

functions.name: StigmergySink.close
functions.description: No-op teardown for the Stigmergy placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is closing.

functions.name: ApprenticeSink.start
functions.description: No-op initialization for the Apprentice placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is starting.
postconditions: The sink is marked as started.

functions.name: ApprenticeSink.emit
functions.description: Placeholder: logs at DEBUG level the story_id and event count that would be emitted as a training sequence to Apprentice. In a real implementation, this would make an HTTP POST to the Apprentice service.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The closed story to emit as a training sequence.

postconditions: A DEBUG log line has been emitted describing the story that would be sent.

structured_side_effects.description: Logs placeholder emit at DEBUG: story_id, event count.

functions.name: ApprenticeSink.close
functions.description: No-op teardown for the Apprentice placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is closing.

functions.name: KindexSink.start
functions.description: No-op initialization for the Kindex placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is starting with the active filter configuration.
preconditions: KindexSink was constructed with a valid KindexSinkConfig.
postconditions: The sink is marked as started.
The active KindexSinkConfig has been logged at DEBUG level.

structured_side_effects.description: Logs start at DEBUG level including filter config summary.

functions.name: KindexSink.emit
functions.description: Evaluates whether the story is noteworthy using is_noteworthy(), and if so, logs at DEBUG what would be emitted as a knowledge node. Stories that do not pass the filter are silently skipped (no error). In a real implementation, noteworthy stories would be posted to the Kindex service.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The closed story to evaluate and potentially emit.

postconditions: If is_noteworthy(story, config) returned true, a DEBUG log line describes the knowledge node that would be emitted.
If not noteworthy, the story is silently dropped with no side effects beyond an optional TRACE/DEBUG log.

structured_side_effects.description: Logs filtered emit decision at DEBUG: story_id, noteworthy flag, filter criteria matched.

functions.name: KindexSink.close
functions.description: No-op teardown for the Kindex placeholder sink. Logs at DEBUG that the sink is closing.

functions.name: is_noteworthy
functions.description: Pure predicate function that evaluates whether a story meets the KindexSinkConfig thresholds for emission as a knowledge node. Extracted as a standalone function for direct unit testability without needing the full KindexSink.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: The filter configuration specifying noteworthy thresholds.

error_cases.name: invalid_timestamps
error_cases.condition: story.opened_at or story.closed_at cannot be parsed as ISO-8601 timestamps.

preconditions: story has a valid closed_at timestamp (it is a closed story).
postconditions: Returns true if and only if all of the following hold: (1) config.story_types is None OR story.story_type is in config.story_types, (2) len(story.events) >= config.min_event_count, (3) (story.closed_at - story.opened_at) in seconds >= config.min_duration_seconds.

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function with no side effects.

functions.name: safe_emit
functions.description: Orchestrator wrapper (defined in engine.py, contracted here as part of the sink fan-out protocol) that calls sink.emit(story) inside a try/except Exception block. On exception, logs a structured error with sink name and story ID and returns an error outcome. Used by asyncio.gather for concurrent fan-out to all registered sinks.

inputs.name: sink_name
inputs.description: Canonical name of the sink for logging and outcome tracking.

inputs.name: sink_emit
inputs.description: The bound async callable sink.emit to invoke (typed as Callable[[Story], Awaitable[None]] at runtime).

preconditions: The sink has been started via start().
story is a valid closed Story.
postconditions: Returns SafeEmitOutcome with result='emitted' if emit succeeded.
Returns SafeEmitOutcome with result='error' and error_message populated if emit raised an exception.
Never raises an exception itself — all errors are captured in the outcome.

structured_side_effects.description: On error: logs structured warning with sink_name, story_id, exception type, and message.

invariants: All sink implementations satisfy the @runtime_checkable SinkProtocol with async methods: emit(story: Story) -> None, start() -> None, close() -> None.
A failing sink's emit() call never blocks, delays, or prevents other sinks from receiving the same story.
Sinks propagate exceptions from emit() to the caller; they do NOT internally swallow errors. Error handling is centralized in the orchestrator's safe_emit wrapper.
DiskSink writes are serialized via asyncio.Lock — concurrent emit() calls produce well-formed, non-interleaved JSONL lines.
DiskSink file is opened in append mode; existing data is never overwritten or truncated.
read_stories never raises for corrupted/unparseable lines; it logs and skips them, yielding only valid Story objects.
KindexSink only emits stories for which is_noteworthy(story, config) returns true.
is_noteworthy is a pure function with no side effects — all filtering logic is testable without I/O.
Placeholder sinks (stigmergy, apprentice, kindex emit action) log at DEBUG level only and perform no network I/O.
All config models (DiskSinkConfig, KindexSinkConfig) are frozen Pydantic v2 models (immutable after construction).
start() must be called before emit(); emit() after close() raises RuntimeError for stateful sinks (DiskSink).
safe_emit never raises — it captures all exceptions into SafeEmitOutcome.error_message.

data_access.rationale: Disk sink writes closed Story objects (containing correlated events with their payloads) to JSONL files on local disk for persistence and audit. Read-back deserializes those same JSONL files. Stigmergy, apprentice, and kindex sinks emit story summaries to external systems. Event payloads may contain INTERNAL-tier operational data (trace IDs, alert metadata, incident fields). No PII is expected in the event model but payloads are opaque so INTERNAL classification applies.

side_effects.rationale: DiskSink appends serialized Story JSON lines to a JSONL file on local disk for durable persistence (AC7).
read_stories reads back previously persisted JSONL story files for deserialization and replay.
StigmergySink emits story summaries as Signals to the Stigmergy service over HTTP (placeholder, logs at DEBUG).
ApprenticeSink emits story event sequences as training data to the Apprentice service (placeholder, logs at DEBUG).
KindexSink emits noteworthy stories filtered by config thresholds as knowledge nodes (placeholder, logs at DEBUG, per AC17).
